SAUL LOEB/Getty Images THE INDICATOR FROM PLANET MONEY Why Trump wants to rip up his own trade deal July 1, 2026 • During President Donald Trump’s first term, he touted the USMCA as the largest and most fair trade deal ever achieved. So why is he now talking about ‘terminating’ it? LISTEN· 9:30 PLAYLIST Download Embed Chris Averyt/City of Spokane SHORT WAVE Is setting your trash on fire a good idea? July 1, 2026 • The U.S. generates 292 million tons of trash each year – and that trash has to go somewhere. Sometimes, that’s to an incinerator, where it’s burned and turned into electricity. Proponents of incineration applaud the ability to generate energy from waste and divert garbage from landfills.

Opponents worry about the potential health risks, especially to minoritized communities. Today on the show, we explore the good and the bad of waste-to-energy facilities – and even get to see one up close. Now, the 40-year wait is over. Manu Fernandez/AP SOCCER EDITION Win or go home: The U.S. to face Bosnia and Herzegovina in World Cup knockout game July 1, 2026 • The U.S. men's team is favored in Wednesday's must-win Round of 32 match — but they haven't beaten a European team since 2021, nor won a World Cup knockout game since 2002. LISTEN· 3:50 PLAYLIST Download Embed Kevin J.

Beaty/Denverite POLITICS Democratic socialist Melat Kiros poised to become the first Gen Z woman in Congress July 1, 2026 • Kiros, a political newcomer, topped 15-term-incumbent Diana DeGette in the Democratic primary in Colorado's 1st Congressional District, a reliably blue House seat. Tasos Katopodis/Getty Images LAW What the Supreme Court did on the final day of its term June 30, 2026 • The Supreme Court upheld the right of children born on U.S. soil to automatic American citizenship. In so doing, the court rejected President Trump's most aggressive attempt to limit immigration.

July 1, 2026 • The U.S. generates 292 million tons of trash each year – and that trash has to go somewhere. Sometimes, that’s to an incinerator, where it’s burned and turned into electricity. Proponents of incineration applaud the ability to generate energy from waste and divert garbage from landfills.

Opponents worry about the potential health risks, especially to minoritized communities. Today on the show, we explore the good and the bad of waste-to-energy facilities – and even get to see one up close.
